mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to separate bull/bear outcomes with a t-stat > 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand recovery, driving sustained positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST yields negative 60-day forward returns in > 60% of observations, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during periods of low inflation signals healthy capacity utilization without pricing pressure, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power when CPIAUCSL exceeds 0.3% monthly growth, or forward returns turn negative in > 50% of low-inflation expansion months.

mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the opportunity cost of holding equities relative to risk-free assets, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 65% of rate hike months, or the correlation between FEDFUNDS changes and SPX returns exceeds -0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll growth combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and generates positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal yields negative 60-day returns in > 55% of cases, or the interaction effect is indistinguishable from noise in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Declining real GDP growth alongside falling housing starts indicates a synchronized domestic demand contraction, forcing equity valuations lower over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict downturns during late-cycle phases with a Rank IC > 0.04.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month drop in industrial production exceeding 0.5% signals abrupt supply-side weakness, triggering immediate risk aversion and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of triggered months, or the threshold signal shows no statistical edge over a buy-and-hold baseline.
